verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein in the immunoglobulin superfamily. In mature spermatozoa, IZUMO1 resides in the inner acrosomal membrane (not yet surface-exposed). Upon the acrosome reaction (triggered during fertilization), IZUMO1 relocates to the sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet, where it mediates sperm-egg fusion by binding oocyte JUNO. Its surface display is therefore restricted to a narrow germline/reproductive cell type (sperm post-acrosome reaction), qualifying as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface rather than constitutive broad surface expression.
